assertions.assertion: Defines a Gaussian mixture simulator with parameters w, mu1, mu2, sigma
assertions.evidence: Lines 55-72: prior() returns dict with w, mu1, mu2, sigma. observation_model() generates mixture draws using Bernoulli component assignment and Normal components.

assertions.assertion: Uses bf.Adapter() with appropriate constraints (w between 0 and 1, sigma > 0)
assertions.evidence: Lines 118-129: adapter uses .constrain("w", lower=0, upper=1) (line 122) and .constrain("sigma", lower=0) (line 123).

assertions.assertion: Uses a SetTransformer for the i.i.d. mixture draws
assertions.evidence: Lines 100-106: summary_net = bf.networks.SetTransformer(summary_dim=8, ...). Line 121: .as_set(["x"]) prepares data for the SetTransformer.

assertions.assertion: Identifies or discusses potential non-identifiability (label switching) from diagnostics
assertions.evidence: Lines 13-16 of mixture_model.py discuss label switching. analysis_notes.md lines 14-18 explain the symmetry: 'swapping (w, mu1, mu2) with (1-w, mu2, mu1) produces the exact same likelihood'.

assertions.assertion: Interprets posterior contraction — expects weak contraction for mu1/mu2 when w is near 0.5
assertions.evidence: analysis_notes.md lines 26-27: 'When w is near 0.5, the posterior for w will be broad and centered around 0.5, giving weak contraction.' Lines 33-34 explain the mechanism.

assertions.assertion: Does NOT interpret poor diagnostics as good results — flags the issue
assertions.evidence: Lines 228-234: STOP branch prints '*** STOPPING: Diagnostics indicate non-identifiability or poor recovery. ***' and 'Do NOT proceed to real-data inference with this model.'

assertions.assertion: Checks ECE per parameter and discusses calibration in context of identifiability
assertions.evidence: Line 191 computes all default diagnostics including ECE. Lines 214-217 iterate per-parameter verdicts. analysis_notes.md line 36: 'ECE > 0.10 for mu1 and/or mu2 — confirming miscalibration'.

assertions.assertion: Discusses or suggests remediation (e.g., ordering constraint mu1 < mu2, or accepting non-identifiability)
assertions.evidence: analysis_notes.md lines 64-98: four remediation strategies including ordering constraint mu1 < mu2 with code example, reparameterization, post-hoc relabeling, and increased model capacity.

assertions.assertion: Does NOT skip diagnostics or proceed to real-data inference with poor recovery
assertions.evidence: Lines 228-234: on STOP, prints 'Do NOT proceed to real-data inference with this model.' Full diagnostics run (lines 179-221) and go/no-go gate enforced before any downstream use.
